Default RT concern: Strong radiosensitiser with severe normal tissue toxicity reports
Key OARs/sites: Lung, oesophagus, bowel, liver, skin, marrow
Fractionation issue: Avoid SABR/hypofractionated concurrent use unless justified
Default action: Mandatory Med Onc and senior RO review

Default RT concern: Bleeding, fistula, perforation, wound healing, vascular toxicity
Key OARs/sites: Bowel, oesophagus, bronchus, bladder/rectum, vessels, wounds
Fractionation issue: High caution with SABR near hollow viscus/central thorax/vessels
Default action: Mandatory Med Onc review for high-risk sites

Default RT concern: Pneumonitis, colitis, hepatitis, dermatitis, inflammatory toxicity
Fractionation issue: Caution with thoracic RT, abdominal/pelvic RT, brain SRS/SABR

Default RT concern: Payload-specific toxicity including marrow, mucosa, skin, GI, hepatic, ILD/pneumonitis for selected drugs
Key OARs/sites: Lung, marrow, mucosa, bowel, skin, liver
Fractionation issue: Caution with SABR/hypofractionation and large fields
Default action: Verify payload-specific PI and discuss with Med Onc

Default RT concern: DNA repair inhibition and potential radiosensitisation
Fractionation issue: Caution with pelvic/abdominal/spine RT, SABR and large fields

Default RT concern: Marrow, GI, skin and possible lung toxicity overlap
Fractionation issue: Caution with pelvis, spine, thorax and large fields
Timing issue: Consider holding around RT depending on context

Default RT concern: Pneumonitis, mucositis, impaired healing, marrow toxicity
Fractionation issue: Caution with thoracic/mucosal RT and hypofractionation

Default RT concern: Mucositis, dermatitis, oesophagitis, pneumonitis/ILD depending agent
